title: Neonauclea monocephala
intro: Neonauclea monocephala, also known as the "Monkey Cup," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical forests of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of various diseases, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Neonauclea monocephala contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Neonauclea monocephala is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Neonauclea monocephala is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The genus Neonauclea is part of the tribe Naucleae, and it is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. This classification places Neonauclea monocephala within the broader context of the Rubiales order and the larger clade of asterids. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the most recent phylogenetic analysis. The classification of Neonauclea monocephala is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
